regimens.site: Stage II (Grade 4 clear cell histology ± sarcomatoid features), Stage III clear cell histology
regimens.setting: Adjuvant

drugs.name: Pembrolizumab
drugs.schedule: Every 3 weeks for up to 1 year

trial_data.os_data: HR for death, 0.62; 95% CI, 0.44–0.87; P = .005 (at median follow-up of 57.2 months)
trial_data.pfs_data: HR for recurrence or death, 0.68; 95% CI, 0.53–0.87; P = .002

regimens.notes: For patients with locoregional clear cell RCC with intermediate-to-high or high risk of recurrence after nephrectomy. Pembrolizumab and berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV pembrolizumab. [100, 101, 102]

regimens.site: Stage II (Grade 4 clear cell histology ± sarcomatoid features), Stage III clear cell histology, Resectable Stage IV (T4, M0) clear cell histology, After metastasectomy for Stage IV/relapsed clear cell histology
regimens.setting: Adjuvant

drugs.name: Pembrolizumab
drugs.schedule: Every 3 weeks for up to 17 cycles

regimens.notes: Added in Version 1.2027 for Grade 4 tumors with clear cell histology ± sarcomatoid features in Stage II, Stage III, and resectable T4, M0 disease. Also an option after metastasectomy with complete resection of disease for Stage IV/relapsed ccRCC. Pembrolizumab and berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV pembrolizumab. [Update]

regimens.setting: First-line (for unresectable Stage IV or relapsed disease)
regimens.notes: For patients with advanced RCC across all risk groups. Pembrolizumab and berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV pembrolizumab. [130, 131, 132, 133]

regimens.setting: First-line (for unresectable Stage IV or relapsed disease)
regimens.notes: For patients with advanced RCC across all risk groups. Nivolumab and hyaluronidase-nvhy subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV nivolumab. [134, 135, 137]

regimens.setting: First-line (for unresectable Stage IV or relapsed disease)

drugs.name: Nivolumab
drugs.schedule: Every 3 weeks for 4 doses (concurrent with ipilimumab)

trial_data.os_data: Ipi/Nivo: 52.7 months; Sunitinib: 37.8 months; HR, 0.72 (95% CI, 0.62–0.83)
trial_data.pfs_data: Ipi/Nivo: 12.4 months; Sunitinib: 12.3 months; HR, 0.88 (95% CI, 0.75–1.03)
trial_data.orr_data: Intermediate-/poor-risk: Ipi/Nivo: 42.4% (95% CI, 37.6–47.2); Sunitinib: 27.5% (95% CI, 23.3–32). Favorable-risk: Ipi/Nivo: 29.6% (95% CI, 21.8–38.4); Sunitinib: 51.6% (95% CI, 42.5%–60.7%)

regimens.notes: For patients with intermediate- or poor-risk advanced RCC (coprimary endpoints), with exploratory analysis for favorable-risk. Nivolumab and hyaluronidase-nvhy is not approved for concurrent use with IV ipilimumab; however, for nivolumab monotherapy, nivolumab and hyaluronidase-nvhy may be substituted. [138, 139, 140]

regimens.setting: First-line (for unresectable Stage IV or relapsed disease)
regimens.notes: For patients with advanced RCC across all risk groups. Pembrolizumab and berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph subcutaneous injection may be substituted for IV pembrolizumab. [141, 142, 143]

trial_data.pfs_data: Cabo: 8.2 months (95% CI, 6.2–8.8); Sunitinib: 5.6 months (95% CI, 3.4–8.1)

regimens.notes: Category 2A, preferred for poor-/intermediate-risk. Category 2B, other recommended for favorable-risk. [144, 145]

regimens.site: Non-clear cell histology (papillary RCC including HLRCC-associated RCC)
regimens.setting: First-line or subsequent therapy

trial_data.os_data: HLRCC: 44.6 months (95% CI, 32.7–NE); Sporadic papillary: 18.2 months (95% CI, 12.6–29.3)
trial_data.pfs_data: HLRCC: 21.1 months (95% CI, 15.6–26.6); Sporadic papillary: 8.9 months (95% CI, 5.5–8.3)

regimens.notes: For select patients with advanced papillary RCC, including HLRCC-associated RCC. An FDA-approved biosimilar is an appropriate substitute for bevacizumab. There are no specific FDA-approved therapies for HLRCC. [205]

regimens.notes: For patients with unresectable advanced or metastatic nccRCC who have not previously received prior systemic therapy. [206]

regimens.setting: First-line or subsequent therapy

drugs.name: Nivolumab
drugs.schedule: Every 3 weeks for 4 doses (concurrent with ipilimumab)

trial_data.os_data: OS rate at 12 months was 78% (95% CI, 71–84) with ipilimumab + nivolumab versus 68% (95% CI, 60–75) with standard of care (P = .026). Median OS: Ipi/Nivo: 33.2 months; SOC: 25.2 months
trial_data.pfs_data: Median PFS: Ipi/Nivo: 5.4 months; SOC: 5.7 months

regimens.notes: For patients with untreated, advanced nccRCC. Nivolumab and hyaluronidase-nvhy is not approved for concurrent use with IV ipilimumab; however, for nivolumab monotherapy, nivolumab and hyaluronidase-nvhy may be substituted. [219]

regimens.regimen_name: Platinum-based chemotherapy (Renal Medullary Carcinoma/Collecting Duct Carcinoma)
regimens.site: Renal medullary carcinoma (RMC) or collecting duct carcinoma
regimens.setting: First-line (preferred outside of clinical trials)

biomarkers.marker: Renal medullary carcinoma or collecting duct histology

trial_data.n_patients: 23 (for gemcitabine/cisplatin or carboplatin in collecting duct)
trial_data.os_data: Collecting duct: Median OS was 10.5 months

regimens.notes: For patients with RMC or collecting duct carcinoma. Partial responses have been observed with cytotoxic chemotherapy (gemcitabine/carboplatin, paclitaxel/carboplatin, or gemcitabine/cisplatin) and other platinum-based chemotherapies. Doxorubicin/gemcitabine can also produce responses in RMC. Oral anti-angiogenic targeted therapies generally do not produce responses in patients with RMC. Erlotinib + bevacizumab can produce responses even in heavily pretreated patients with RMC. Immune checkpoint therapies (ipilimumab + nivolumab or pembrolizumab) should be avoided in RMC outside of a clinical trial as they have demonstrated limited efficacy and may be associated with rapid disease progression. Outside of clinical trials, platinum-based chemotherapy regimens should be the preferred first-line therapy for RMC. [Update, 227, 228, 229]

regimens.setting: First-line or subsequent therapy (including heavily pretreated)

trial_data.n_patients: 43 with HLRCC-associated RCC (which includes aggressive papillary histology similar to RMC)

regimens.notes: Cited as an option that can produce responses even in heavily pretreated patients with RMC. An FDA-approved biosimilar is an appropriate substitute for bevacizumab. [205]

regimens.setting: First-line (for asymptomatic, growing angiomyolipoma >3 cm)

trial_data.orr_data: Confirmed angiomyolipoma response was reported in 58% of patients, and ≥75% had a persistent angiomyolipoma volume decrease of at least 30%

regimens.notes: For patients with TSC-associated benign renal angiomyolipomas not requiring immediate surgery. [255, 256]

regimens.setting: First-line (alternative to everolimus)

trial_data.orr_data: Angiomyolipoma volume was reduced by ≥30% for 16 out of 20 patients assessed at 12 months

regimens.notes: For patients with TSC or sporadic lymphangioleiomyomatosis. [257]

regimens.setting: First-line (for VHL-associated RCC not requiring immediate surgery)

trial_data.orr_data: ORR was 67% at 50-month follow-up, with 7 CR and 34 PR

regimens.notes: FDA-approved for the treatment of VHL disease-associated RCC, CNS hemangioblastomas, or pNET, not requiring immediate surgery. [258, 259]

regimens.setting: First-line (for VHL-associated nonmetastatic lesions)

trial_data.orr_data: 42% ORR and 52% renal tumor-specific response rate

regimens.notes: For patients with VHL disease-associated nonmetastatic lesions. [260]
